programs.name: SNAP (Idaho)
programs.description: Idaho administers federal SNAP with standard federal eligibility rules.

monthly_benefit_range.note: Standard federal benefit amounts (FY2025 maximum allotments)

income.note: Federal gross income limit; net income must be at or below 100% FPL

programs.name: Idaho Medicaid
programs.description: Idaho has expanded Medicaid under the ACA. Adults are covered up to 138% FPL. Children and pregnant women have higher income limits (CHIP up to 185% FPL).

monthly_benefit_range.note: No premium for most enrollees. Covers doctor visits, hospital, prescriptions, and more.

required_documents: Social Security number or immigration documents

programs.name: Temporary Assistance for Families in Idaho (TAFI)
programs.full_name: Temporary Assistance for Families in Idaho (TAFI) — Temporary Assistance for Needy Families
programs.description: Idaho's TANF program provides time-limited cash assistance to low-income families with children. Maximum monthly benefit for a family of 3 is approximately $309.

eligibility_rules.who: Families with dependent children under 18 (or 19 if still in high school)

income.note: Very low income limits; varies by family size. Generally well below 100% FPL.

eligibility_rules.time_limit: 60-month lifetime limit (federal); some states have shorter limits
